reasoning: The base rate of a US president resigning is exceptionally low (only Nixon has ever done so), equating to less than a 1% probability per year. Furthermore, Donald Trump’s well-established personality profile and historical behavior strongly suggest he is extremely unlikely to ever voluntarily relinquish power or admit defeat. He did not resign during either of his impeachments and refused to concede the 2020 election. There are currently no credible rumors, news reports, or medical emergencies suggesting he is considering stepping down in 2025. Although the Polymarket price was 5% on March 6, 2025, longshot political markets on Polymarket consistently overprice highly unlikely events due to longshot bias, minimum tick sizes, and locked capital effects. Therefore, the true probability is much lower than the market price.
